title: Move (procedural memory unit / SKILL.md frontmatter)
description: One atomic procedural-memory unit — the v2 substrate's Layer 3a. External format: SKILL.md per agentskills.io. This schema describes the YAML frontmatter Trinity reads + writes; the body (post-frontmatter markdown) is the actual procedure and is out-of-band. Trinity adds `trinity_*` extension fields that the SKILL.md spec explicitly allows. Lives on disk at `~/.trinity/moves/<slug>/SKILL.md` (active) or `~/.trinity/moves/archive/<slug>/SKILL.md` (demoted). Sufficient on its own — `name + description` are the agentskills.io-required minimum; every other field is Trinity-specific and optional. Tools that read Trinity's substrate but don't care about Bayesian state can safely ignore everything past `description`.

name.description: Human-readable move title. Required by the SKILL.md spec. Trinity slugifies this to a kebab-case directory name for filesystem storage but the canonical identity is the prose name.

description.description: One-line description. Required by the SKILL.md spec. Agents reading the substrate use this for activation-time matching.

trinity_promoted_from.description: Rejection-signal ids (from `~/.trinity/me/rejections.jsonl`) that seeded this move. Provenance — lets users trace a move back to the user_substitute exemplars dream proposed it from.

trinity_basin_id.description: Basin id (from `~/.trinity/memories/topics.json`) the move serves. Moves are basin-scoped — a move that fires on basin A doesn't apply to basin B.

trinity_promoted_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this move cleared the T1+T2+T3 gate and landed on disk.

trinity_demoted_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when this move was demoted to archive. Null on active moves. Co-defined with `trinity_demoted_by_tier` — they appear together or not at all.

trinity_demoted_by_tier.description: Which tier triggered the demotion. T4 = posterior drifted below baseline (the only tier that demotes today). Null on active moves.

trinity_alpha.description: Beta-Binomial conjugate prior — chairman-picked successes + 1 (uninformative prior). Increments via `record_success`. Posterior = alpha / (alpha + beta).

trinity_beta.description: Beta-Binomial conjugate prior — chairman-not-picked failures + 1. Increments via `record_failure`.

trinity_execution_count.description: Total councils where this move was applied (alpha + beta - 2, excluding the uninformative prior). Used as the `min_executions` guard before T4 demotion bites.

trinity_t1_lexical_score.description: Most recent T1 (lexical Jaccard) score from the gate. Persisted so users can inspect why a move was promoted.

trinity_t2_embedding_score.description: Most recent T2 (embedding cosine vs basin centroid) score.

trinity_t3_chairman_score.description: Most recent T3 (chairman eval against rejection corpus) score.

trinity_eval_baseline.description: The T3 score captured at first promotion. Re-evaluations gate against this baseline — drift below it triggers demotion.

trinity_success_contexts.description: Basins where this move fired correctly. MACLA contrastive-learning surface — lets users see where a move generalizes.

trinity_failure_contexts.description: Basins where this move fired incorrectly. Useful when the same move is promoted in basin A but mis-fires when applied in basin B.

trinity_generalizability_score.description: How broadly this move applies across basins (0..1). Independent of confidence — a move can be high-confidence in one basin AND low-generalizability simultaneously.

trinity_lens_tensions_addressed.description: Count of lens.md tension pairs this move addresses. Helps users prioritize which moves serve their most-load-bearing preferences.
